message:
feat(raptorq): wavefront decode pipeline for bounded assembly+peeling

Add `decode_wavefront(symbols, batch_size)` that fuses assembly and
peeling into bounded batches — symbols are assembled in chunks of
`batch_size`, and after each chunk the peeling queue is drained via the
new factored-out `peel_from_queue()` method. This reduces pipeline
bubbles by allowing degree-1 equations discovered early to be solved
while remaining symbols are still being assembled.

New `DecodeStats` fields: `wavefront_active`, `wavefront_batches`,
`wavefront_overlap_peeled`, `wavefront_batch_size`.

Four comprehensive tests:
- `wavefront_decode_matches_sequential` (batch sizes 1/2/4/8/16/0)
- `wavefront_decode_with_loss_matches_sequential` (repair-only decode)
- `wavefront_overlap_peeling_is_tracked` (batch_size=1 overlap metrics)
- `wavefront_sequential_fallback_batch_zero` (batch_size=0 equivalence)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
